2547 if center is None:
2548 center = (w / 2, h / 2)
2549  
2550 angle = -math.radians(angle)
2551 matrix = [
2552 round(math.cos(angle), 15),
2553 round(math.sin(angle), 15),
2554 0.0,
2555 round(-math.sin(angle), 15),
2556 round(math.cos(angle), 15),
2557 0.0,
2558 ]
2559  
2560 def transform(x: float, y: float, matrix: list[float]) -> tuple[float, float]:
2561 a, b, c, d, e, f = matrix
2562 return a * x + b * y + c, d * x + e * y + f
2563  
2564 matrix[2], matrix[5] = transform(
2565 -center[0] - post_trans[0], -center[1] - post_trans[1], matrix
2566 )
2567 matrix[2] += center[0]
2568 matrix[5] += center[1]
2569  
2570 if expand:
2571 # calculate output size
2572 xx = []
2573 yy = []
2574 for x, y in ((0, 0), (w, 0), (w, h), (0, h)):
2575 transformed_x, transformed_y = transform(x, y, matrix)
2576 xx.append(transformed_x)
2577 yy.append(transformed_y)
2578 nw = math.ceil(max(xx)) - math.floor(min(xx))
2579 nh = math.ceil(max(yy)) - math.floor(min(yy))
2580  
2581 # We multiply a translation matrix from the right. Because of its
2582 # special form, this is the same as taking the image of the
2583 # translation vector as new translation vector.
2584 matrix[2], matrix[5] = transform(-(nw - w) / 2.0, -(nh - h) / 2.0, matrix)
2585 w, h = nw, nh
2586  
2587 return self.transform(
2588 (w, h), Transform.AFFINE, matrix, resample, fillcolor=fillcolor
2589 )
2590  
2591 def save(
2592 self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
2593 ) -> None:
2594 """
2595 Saves this image under the given filename. If no format is
2596 specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
2597 extension, if possible.
2598  
2599 Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
2600 to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
2601 silently ignored. The available options are described in the
2602 :doc:`image format documentation
2603 <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.
2604  
2605 You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
2606 you must always specify the format. The file object must
2607 implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
2608 methods, and be opened in binary mode.
2609  
2610 :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
2611 :param format: Optional format override. If omitted, the
2612 format to use is determined from the filename extension.
2613 If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
2614 parameter should always be used.
2615 :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
2616 set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
2617 saving multiple images::
2618  
2619 # Saving XMP data to a single image
2620 from PIL import Image
2621 red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
2622 red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")
2623  
2624 # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
2625 from PIL import Image
2626 black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
2627 red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
2628 red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
2629 black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])
2630 :returns: None
2631 :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
2632 from the file name. Use the format option to solve this.
2633 :exception OSError: If the file could not be written. The file
2634 may have been created, and may contain partial data.
2635 """
2636  
2637 filename: str | bytes = ""
2638 open_fp = False
2639 if is_path(fp):
2640 filename = os.fspath(fp)
2641 open_fp = True
2642 elif fp == sys.stdout:
2643 try:
2644 fp = sys.stdout.buffer
2645 except AttributeError:
2646 pass
2647 if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
2648 # only set the name for metadata purposes
2649 filename = os.fspath(fp.name)
2650  
2651 if format:
2652 preinit()
2653 else:
2654 filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
2655 ext = (
2656 filename_ext.decode()
2657 if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes)
2658 else filename_ext
2659 )
2660  
2661 # Try importing only the plugin for this extension first
2662 if not _import_plugin_for_extension(ext):
2663 preinit()
2664  
2665 if ext not in EXTENSION:
2666 init()
2667 try:
2668 format = EXTENSION[ext]
2669 except KeyError as e:
2670 msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
2671 raise ValueError(msg) from e
2672  
2673 from . import ImageFile
2674  
2675 # may mutate self!
2676 if isinstance(self, ImageFile.ImageFile) and os.path.abspath(
2677 filename
2678 ) == os.path.abspath(self.filename):
2679 self._ensure_mutable()
2680 else:
2681 self.load()
2682  
2683 save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
2684 self._default_encoderinfo = params
2685 encoderinfo = getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {})
2686 self._attach_default_encoderinfo(self)
2687 self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()
2688  
2689 if format.upper() not in SAVE:
2690 init()
2691 if save_all or (
2692 save_all is None
2693 and params.get("append_images")
2694 and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
2695 ):
2696 save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
2697 else:
2698 save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]
2699  
2700 created = False
2701 if open_fp:
2702 created = not os.path.exists(filename)
2703 if params.get("append", False):
2704 # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
2705 # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
2706 fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
2707 else:
2708 fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
2709 else:
2710 fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)
2711  
2712 try:
2713 save_handler(self, fp, filename)
2714 except Exception:
2715 if open_fp:
2716 fp.close()
2717 if created:
2718 try:
2719 os.remove(filename)
2720 except PermissionError:
2721 pass
2722 raise
2723 finally:
2724 self.encoderinfo = encoderinfo
2725 if open_fp:
2726 fp.close()
2727  
2728 def _attach_default_encoderinfo(self, im: Image) -> dict[str, Any]:
2729 encoderinfo = getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {})
2730 self.encoderinfo = {**im._default_encoderinfo, **encoderinfo}
2731 return encoderinfo
2732  
2733 def seek(self, frame: int) -> None:
2734 """
2735 Seeks to the given frame in this sequence file. If you seek
2736 beyond the end of the sequence, the method raises an
2737 ``EOFError`` exception. When a sequence file is opened, the
2738 library automatically seeks to frame 0.
2739  
2740 See :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.tell`.
2741  
2742 If defined, :attr:`~PIL.Image.Image.n_frames` refers to the
2743 number of available frames.
2744  
2745 :param frame: Frame number, starting at 0.
2746 :exception EOFError: If the call attempts to seek beyond the end
2747 of the sequence.
2748 """
2749  
2750 # overridden by file handlers
2751 if frame != 0:
2752 msg = "no more images in file"
2753 raise EOFError(msg)
2754  
2755 def show(self, title: str | None = None) -> None:
2756 """
2757 Displays this image. This method is mainly intended for debugging purposes.
2758  
2759 This method calls :py:func:`PIL.ImageShow.show` internally. You can use
2760 :py:func:`PIL.ImageShow.register` to override its default behaviour.
2761  
2762 The image is first saved to a temporary file. By default, it will be in
2763 PNG format.
2764  
2765 On Unix, the image is then opened using the **xdg-open**, **display**,
2766 **gm**, **eog** or **xv** utility, depending on which one can be found.
2767  
2768 On macOS, the image is opened with the native Preview application.
2769  
2770 On Windows, the image is opened with the standard PNG display utility.
2771  
2772 :param title: Optional title to use for the image window, where possible.
2773 """
2774  
2775 from . import ImageShow
2776  
2777 ImageShow.show(self, title)
2778  
2779 def split(self) -> tuple[Image, ...]:
2780 """
2781 Split this image into individual bands. This method returns a
2782 tuple of individual image bands from an image. For example,
2783 splitting an "RGB" image creates three new images each
2784 containing a copy of one of the original bands (red, green,
2785 blue).
2786  
2787 If you need only one band, :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.getchannel`
2788 method can be more convenient and faster.
2789  
2790 :returns: A tuple containing bands.
2791 """
2792  
2793 self.load()
2794 if self.im.bands == 1:
2795 return (self.copy(),)
2796 return tuple(map(self._new, self.im.split()))
2797  
2798 def getchannel(self, channel: int | str) -> Image:
2799 """
2800 Returns an image containing a single channel of the source image.
2801  
2802 :param channel: What channel to return. Could be index
2803 (0 for "R" channel of "RGB") or channel name
2804 ("A" for alpha channel of "RGBA").
2805 :returns: An image in "L" mode.
2806  
2807 .. versionadded:: 4.3.0
2808 """
2809 self.load()
2810  
2811 if isinstance(channel, str):
2812 try:
2813 channel = self.getbands().index(channel)
2814 except ValueError as e:
2815 msg = f'The image has no channel "{channel}"'
2816 raise ValueError(msg) from e
2817  
2818 return self._new(self.im.getband(channel))
2819  
2820 def tell(self) -> int:
2821 """
2822 Returns the current frame number. See :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.seek`.
2823  
2824 If defined, :attr:`~PIL.Image.Image.n_frames` refers to the
2825 number of available frames.
2826  
2827 :returns: Frame number, starting with 0.
2828 """
2829 return 0
2830  
2831 def thumbnail(
2832 self,
2833 size: tuple[float, float],
2834 resample: Resampling = Resampling.BICUBIC,
2835 reducing_gap: float | None = 2.0,
2836 ) -> None:
2837 """
2838 Make this image into a thumbnail. This method modifies the
2839 image to contain a thumbnail version of itself, no larger than
2840 the given size. This method calculates an appropriate thumbnail
2841 size to preserve the aspect of the image, calls the
2842 :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.draft` method to configure the file reader
2843 (where applicable), and finally resizes the image.
2844  
2845 Note that this function modifies the :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image`
2846 object in place. If you need to use the full resolution image as well,
2847 apply this method to a :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.copy` of the original
2848 image.
2849  
2850 :param size: The requested size in pixels, as a 2-tuple:
2851 (width, height).
2852 :param resample: Optional resampling filter. This can be one
2853 of :py:data:`Resampling.NEAREST`, :py:data:`Resampling.BOX`,
2854 :py:data:`Resampling.BILINEAR`, :py:data:`Resampling.HAMMING`,
2855 :py:data:`Resampling.BICUBIC` or :py:data:`Resampling.LANCZOS`.
2856 If omitted, it defaults to :py:data:`Resampling.BICUBIC`.
2857 (was :py:data:`Resampling.NEAREST` prior to version 2.5.0).
2858 See: :ref:`concept-filters`.
2859 :param reducing_gap: Apply optimization by resizing the image
2860 in two steps. First, reducing the image by integer times
2861 using :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.reduce` or
2862 :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.draft` for JPEG images.
2863 Second, resizing using regular resampling. The last step
2864 changes size no less than by ``reducing_gap`` times.
2865 ``reducing_gap`` may be None (no first step is performed)
2866 or should be greater than 1.0. The bigger ``reducing_gap``,
2867 the closer the result to the fair resampling.
2868 The smaller ``reducing_gap``, the faster resizing.
2869 With ``reducing_gap`` greater or equal to 3.0, the result is
2870 indistinguishable from fair resampling in most cases.
2871 The default value is 2.0 (very close to fair resampling
2872 while still being faster in many cases).
2873 :returns: None
2874 """
2875  
2876 provided_size = tuple(map(math.floor, size))
2877  
2878 def preserve_aspect_ratio() -> tuple[int, int] | None:
2879 def round_aspect(number: float, key: Callable[[int], float]) -> int:
2880 return max(min(math.floor(number), math.ceil(number), key=key), 1)
2881  
2882 x, y = provided_size
2883 if x >= self.width and y >= self.height:
2884 return None
2885  
2886 aspect = self.width / self.height
2887 if x / y >= aspect:
2888 x = round_aspect(y * aspect, key=lambda n: abs(aspect - n / y))
2889 else:
2890 y = round_aspect(
2891 x / aspect, key=lambda n: 0 if n == 0 else abs(aspect - x / n)
2892 )
2893 return x, y
2894  
2895 preserved_size = preserve_aspect_ratio()
2896 if preserved_size is None:
2897 return
2898 final_size = preserved_size
2899  
2900 box = None
2901 if reducing_gap is not None:
2902 res = self.draft(
2903 None, (int(size[0] * reducing_gap), int(size[1] * reducing_gap))
2904 )
2905 if res is not None:
2906 box = res[1]
2907  
2908 if self.size != final_size:
2909 im = self.resize(final_size, resample, box=box, reducing_gap=reducing_gap)
2910  
2911 self.im = im.im
2912 self._size = final_size
2913 self._mode = self.im.mode
2914  
2915 self.readonly = 0
2916  
2917 # FIXME: the different transform methods need further explanation
2918 # instead of bloating the method docs, add a separate chapter.
2919 def transform(
2920 self,
2921 size: tuple[int, int],
2922 method: Transform | ImageTransformHandler | SupportsGetData,
2923 data: Sequence[Any] | None = None,
2924 resample: int = Resampling.NEAREST,
2925 fill: int = 1,
2926 fillcolor: float | tuple[float, ...] | str | None = None,
2927 ) -> Image:
2928 """
2929 Transforms this image. This method creates a new image with the
2930 given size, and the same mode as the original, and copies data
2931 to the new image using the given transform.
2932  
2933 :param size: The output size in pixels, as a 2-tuple:
2934 (width, height).
2935 :param method: The transformation method. This is one of
2936 :py:data:`Transform.EXTENT` (cut out a rectangular subregion),
2937 :py:data:`Transform.AFFINE` (affine transform),
2938 :py:data:`Transform.PERSPECTIVE` (perspective transform),
2939 :py:data:`Transform.QUAD` (map a quadrilateral to a rectangle), or
2940 :py:data:`Transform.MESH` (map a number of source quadrilaterals
2941 in one operation).
2942  
2943 It may also be an :py:class:`~PIL.Image.ImageTransformHandler`
2944 object::
2945  
2946 class Example(Image.ImageTransformHandler):
2947 def transform(self, size, data, resample, fill=1):
2948 # Return result
2949  
2950 Implementations of :py:class:`~PIL.Image.ImageTransformHandler`
2951 for some of the :py:class:`Transform` methods are provided
2952 in :py:mod:`~PIL.ImageTransform`.
2953  
2954 It may also be an object with a ``method.getdata`` method
2955 that returns a tuple supplying new ``method`` and ``data`` values::
2956  
2957 class Example:
2958 def getdata(self):
2959 method = Image.Transform.EXTENT
2960 data = (0, 0, 100, 100)
2961 return method, data
2962 :param data: Extra data to the transformation method.
2963 :param resample: Optional resampling filter. It can be one of
2964 :py:data:`Resampling.NEAREST` (use nearest neighbour),
2965 :py:data:`Resampling.BILINEAR` (linear interpolation in a 2x2
2966 environment), or :py:data:`Resampling.BICUBIC` (cubic spline
2967 interpolation in a 4x4 environment). If omitted, or if the image
2968 has mode "1" or "P", it is set to :py:data:`Resampling.NEAREST`.
2969 See: :ref:`concept-filters`.
2970 :param fill: If ``method`` is an
2971 :py:class:`~PIL.Image.ImageTransformHandler` object, this is one of
2972 the arguments passed to it. Otherwise, it is unused.
2973 :param fillcolor: Optional fill color for the area outside the
2974 transform in the output image.
2975 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
2976 """
2977  
2978 if self.mode in ("LA", "RGBA") and resample != Resampling.NEAREST:
2979 return (
2980 self.convert({"LA": "La", "RGBA": "RGBa"}[self.mode])
2981 .transform(size, method, data, resample, fill, fillcolor)
2982 .convert(self.mode)
2983 )
2984  
2985 if isinstance(method, ImageTransformHandler):
2986 return method.transform(size, self, resample=resample, fill=fill)
2987  
2988 if hasattr(method, "getdata"):
2989 # compatibility w. old-style transform objects
2990 method, data = method.getdata()
2991  
2992 if data is None:
2993 msg = "missing method data"
2994 raise ValueError(msg)
2995  
2996 im = new(self.mode, size, fillcolor)
2997 if self.mode == "P" and self.palette:
2998 im.palette = self.palette.copy()
2999 im.info = self.info.copy()
3000 if method == Transform.MESH:
3001 # list of quads
3002 for box, quad in data:
3003 im.__transformer(
3004 box, self, Transform.QUAD, quad, resample, fillcolor is None
3005 )
3006 else:
3007 im.__transformer(
3008 (0, 0) + size, self, method, data, resample, fillcolor is None
3009 )
3010  
3011 return im
3012  
3013 def __transformer(
3014 self,
3015 box: tuple[int, int, int, int],
3016 image: Image,
3017 method: Transform,
3018 data: Sequence[float],
3019 resample: int = Resampling.NEAREST,
3020 fill: bool = True,
3021 ) -> None:
3022 w = box[2] - box[0]
3023 h = box[3] - box[1]
3024  
3025 if method == Transform.AFFINE:
3026 data = data[:6]
3027  
3028 elif method == Transform.EXTENT:
3029 # convert extent to an affine transform
3030 x0, y0, x1, y1 = data
3031 xs = (x1 - x0) / w
3032 ys = (y1 - y0) / h
3033 method = Transform.AFFINE
3034 data = (xs, 0, x0, 0, ys, y0)
3035  
3036 elif method == Transform.PERSPECTIVE:
3037 data = data[:8]
3038  
3039 elif method == Transform.QUAD:
3040 # quadrilateral warp. data specifies the four corners
3041 # given as NW, SW, SE, and NE.
3042 nw = data[:2]
3043 sw = data[2:4]
3044 se = data[4:6]
3045 ne = data[6:8]
3046 x0, y0 = nw
3047 As = 1.0 / w
3048 At = 1.0 / h
3049 data = (
3050 x0,
3051 (ne[0] - x0) * As,
3052 (sw[0] - x0) * At,
3053 (se[0] - sw[0] - ne[0] + x0) * As * At,
3054 y0,
3055 (ne[1] - y0) * As,
3056 (sw[1] - y0) * At,
3057 (se[1] - sw[1] - ne[1] + y0) * As * At,
3058 )
3059  
3060 else:
3061 msg = "unknown transformation method"
3062 raise ValueError(msg)
3063  
3064 if resample not in (
3065 Resampling.NEAREST,
3066 Resampling.BILINEAR,
3067 Resampling.BICUBIC,
3068 ):
3069 if resample in (Resampling.BOX, Resampling.HAMMING, Resampling.LANCZOS):
3070 unusable: dict[int, str] = {
3071 Resampling.BOX: "Image.Resampling.BOX",
3072 Resampling.HAMMING: "Image.Resampling.HAMMING",
3073 Resampling.LANCZOS: "Image.Resampling.LANCZOS",
3074 }
3075 msg = unusable[resample] + f" ({resample}) cannot be used."
3076 else:
3077 msg = f"Unknown resampling filter ({resample})."
3078  
3079 filters = [
3080 f"{filter[1]} ({filter[0]})"
3081 for filter in (
3082 (Resampling.NEAREST, "Image.Resampling.NEAREST"),
3083 (Resampling.BILINEAR, "Image.Resampling.BILINEAR"),
3084 (Resampling.BICUBIC, "Image.Resampling.BICUBIC"),
3085 )
3086 ]
3087 msg += f" Use {', '.join(filters[:-1])} or {filters[-1]}"
3088 raise ValueError(msg)
3089  
3090 image.load()
3091  
3092 self.load()
3093  
3094 if image.mode in ("1", "P"):
3095 resample = Resampling.NEAREST
3096  
3097 self.im.transform(box, image.im, method, data, resample, fill)
3098  
3099 def transpose(self, method: Transpose) -> Image:
3100 """
3101 Transpose image (flip or rotate in 90 degree steps)
3102  
3103 :param method: One of :py:data:`Transpose.FLIP_LEFT_RIGHT`,
3104 :py:data:`Transpose.FLIP_TOP_BOTTOM`, :py:data:`Transpose.ROTATE_90`,
3105 :py:data:`Transpose.ROTATE_180`, :py:data:`Transpose.ROTATE_270`,
3106 :py:data:`Transpose.TRANSPOSE` or :py:data:`Transpose.TRANSVERSE`.
3107 :returns: Returns a flipped or rotated copy of this image.
3108 """
3109  
3110 self.load()
3111 return self._new(self.im.transpose(method))
3112  
3113 def effect_spread(self, distance: int) -> Image:
3114 """
3115 Randomly spread pixels in an image.
3116  
3117 :param distance: Distance to spread pixels.
3118 """
3119 self.load()
3120 return self._new(self.im.effect_spread(distance))
3121  
3122 def toqimage(self) -> ImageQt.ImageQt:
3123 """Returns a QImage copy of this image"""
3124 from . import ImageQt
3125  
3126 if not ImageQt.qt_is_installed:
3127 msg = "Qt bindings are not installed"
3128 raise ImportError(msg)
3129 return ImageQt.toqimage(self)
3130  
3131 def toqpixmap(self) -> ImageQt.QPixmap:
3132 """Returns a QPixmap copy of this image"""
3133 from . import ImageQt
3134  
3135 if not ImageQt.qt_is_installed:
3136 msg = "Qt bindings are not installed"
3137 raise ImportError(msg)
3138 return ImageQt.toqpixmap(self)
3139  
3140  
3141 # --------------------------------------------------------------------
3142 # Abstract handlers.
3143  
3144  
3145 class ImagePointHandler(abc.ABC):
3146 """
3147 Used as a mixin by point transforms
3148 (for use with :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.point`)
3149 """
3150  
3151 @abc.abstractmethod
3152 def point(self, im: Image) -> Image:
3153 pass
3154  
3155  
3156 class ImageTransformHandler(abc.ABC):
3157 """
3158 Used as a mixin by geometry transforms
3159 (for use with :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.transform`)
3160 """
3161  
3162 @abc.abstractmethod
3163 def transform(
3164 self,
3165 size: tuple[int, int],
3166 image: Image,
3167 **options: Any,
3168 ) -> Image:
3169 pass
3170  
3171  
3172 # --------------------------------------------------------------------
3173 # Factories
3174  
3175  
3176 def _check_size(size: Any) -> None:
3177 """
3178 Common check to enforce type and sanity check on size tuples
3179  
3180 :param size: Should be a 2 tuple of (width, height)
3181 :returns: None, or raises a ValueError
3182 """
3183  
3184 if not isinstance(size, (list, tuple)):
3185 msg = "Size must be a list or tuple"
3186 raise ValueError(msg)
3187 if len(size) != 2:
3188 msg = "Size must be a sequence of length 2"
3189 raise ValueError(msg)
3190 if size[0] < 0 or size[1] < 0:
3191 msg = "Width and height must be >= 0"
3192 raise ValueError(msg)
3193  
3194  
3195 def new(
3196 mode: str,
3197 size: tuple[int, int] | list[int],
3198 color: float | tuple[float, ...] | str | None = 0,
3199 ) -> Image:
3200 """
3201 Creates a new image with the given mode and size.
3202  
3203 :param mode: The mode to use for the new image. See:
3204 :ref:`concept-modes`.
3205 :param size: A 2-tuple, containing (width, height) in pixels.
3206 :param color: What color to use for the image. Default is black. If given,
3207 this should be a single integer or floating point value for single-band
3208 modes, and a tuple for multi-band modes (one value per band). When
3209 creating RGB or HSV images, you can also use color strings as supported
3210 by the ImageColor module. See :ref:`colors` for more information. If the
3211 color is None, the image is not initialised.
3212 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3213 """
3214  
3215 _check_size(size)
3216  
3217 if color is None:
3218 # don't initialize
3219 return Image()._new(core.new(mode, size))
3220  
3221 if isinstance(color, str):
3222 # css3-style specifier
3223  
3224 from . import ImageColor
3225  
3226 color = ImageColor.getcolor(color, mode)
3227  
3228 im = Image()
3229 if (
3230 mode == "P"
3231 and isinstance(color, (list, tuple))
3232 and all(isinstance(i, int) for i in color)
3233 ):
3234 color_ints: tuple[int, ...] = cast(tuple[int, ...], tuple(color))
3235 if len(color_ints) == 3 or len(color_ints) == 4:
3236 # RGB or RGBA value for a P image
3237 from . import ImagePalette
3238  
3239 im.palette = ImagePalette.ImagePalette()
3240 color = im.palette.getcolor(color_ints)
3241 return im._new(core.fill(mode, size, color))
3242  
3243  
3244 def frombytes(
3245 mode: str,
3246 size: tuple[int, int],
3247 data: bytes | bytearray | SupportsArrayInterface,
3248 decoder_name: str = "raw",
3249 *args: Any,
3250 ) -> Image:
3251 """
3252 Creates a copy of an image memory from pixel data in a buffer.
3253  
3254 In its simplest form, this function takes three arguments
3255 (mode, size, and unpacked pixel data).
3256  
3257 You can also use any pixel decoder supported by PIL. For more
3258 information on available decoders, see the section
3259 :ref:`Writing Your Own File Codec <file-codecs>`.
3260  
3261 Note that this function decodes pixel data only, not entire images.
3262 If you have an entire image in a string, wrap it in a
3263 :py:class:`~io.BytesIO` object, and use :py:func:`~PIL.Image.open` to load
3264 it.
3265  
3266 :param mode: The image mode. See: :ref:`concept-modes`.
3267 :param size: The image size.
3268 :param data: A byte buffer containing raw data for the given mode.
3269 :param decoder_name: What decoder to use.
3270 :param args: Additional parameters for the given decoder.
3271 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3272 """
3273  
3274 _check_size(size)
3275  
3276 im = new(mode, size)
3277 if im.width != 0 and im.height != 0:
3278 decoder_args: Any = args
3279 if len(decoder_args) == 1 and isinstance(decoder_args[0], tuple):
3280 # may pass tuple instead of argument list
3281 decoder_args = decoder_args[0]
3282  
3283 if decoder_name == "raw" and decoder_args == ():
3284 decoder_args = mode
3285  
3286 im.frombytes(data, decoder_name, decoder_args)
3287 return im
3288  
3289  
3290 def frombuffer(
3291 mode: str,
3292 size: tuple[int, int],
3293 data: bytes | SupportsArrayInterface,
3294 decoder_name: str = "raw",
3295 *args: Any,
3296 ) -> Image:
3297 """
3298 Creates an image memory referencing pixel data in a byte buffer.
3299  
3300 This function is similar to :py:func:`~PIL.Image.frombytes`, but uses data
3301 in the byte buffer, where possible. This means that changes to the
3302 original buffer object are reflected in this image). Not all modes can
3303 share memory; supported modes include "L", "RGBX", "RGBA", and "CMYK".
3304  
3305 Note that this function decodes pixel data only, not entire images.
3306 If you have an entire image file in a string, wrap it in a
3307 :py:class:`~io.BytesIO` object, and use :py:func:`~PIL.Image.open` to load it.
3308  
3309 The default parameters used for the "raw" decoder differs from that used for
3310 :py:func:`~PIL.Image.frombytes`. This is a bug, and will probably be fixed in a
3311 future release. The current release issues a warning if you do this; to disable
3312 the warning, you should provide the full set of parameters. See below for details.
3313  
3314 :param mode: The image mode. See: :ref:`concept-modes`.
3315 :param size: The image size.
3316 :param data: A bytes or other buffer object containing raw
3317 data for the given mode.
3318 :param decoder_name: What decoder to use.
3319 :param args: Additional parameters for the given decoder. For the
3320 default encoder ("raw"), it's recommended that you provide the
3321 full set of parameters::
3322  
3323 frombuffer(mode, size, data, "raw", mode, 0, 1)
3324  
3325 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3326  
3327 .. versionadded:: 1.1.4
3328 """
3329  
3330 _check_size(size)
3331  
3332 # may pass tuple instead of argument list
3333 if len(args) == 1 and isinstance(args[0], tuple):
3334 args = args[0]
3335  
3336 if decoder_name == "raw":
3337 if args == ():
3338 args = mode, 0, 1
3339 if args[0] in _MAPMODES:
3340 im = new(mode, (0, 0))
3341 im = im._new(core.map_buffer(data, size, decoder_name, 0, args))
3342 if mode == "P":
3343 from . import ImagePalette
3344  
3345 im.palette = ImagePalette.ImagePalette("RGB", im.im.getpalette("RGB"))
3346 im.readonly = 1
3347 return im
3348  
3349 return frombytes(mode, size, data, decoder_name, args)
3350  
3351  
3352 class SupportsArrayInterface(Protocol):
3353 """
3354 An object that has an ``__array_interface__`` dictionary.
3355 """
3356  
3357 @property
3358 def __array_interface__(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
3359 raise NotImplementedError()
3360  
3361  
3362 class SupportsArrowArrayInterface(Protocol):
3363 """
3364 An object that has an ``__arrow_c_array__`` method corresponding to the arrow c
3365 data interface.
3366 """
3367  
3368 def __arrow_c_array__(
3369 self, requested_schema: "PyCapsule" = None # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821, UP037
3370 ) -> tuple["PyCapsule", "PyCapsule"]: # type: ignore[name-defined] # noqa: F821, UP037
3371 raise NotImplementedError()
3372  
3373  
3374 def fromarray(obj: SupportsArrayInterface, mode: str | None = None) -> Image:
3375 """
3376 Creates an image memory from an object exporting the array interface
3377 (using the buffer protocol)::
3378  
3379 from PIL import Image
3380 import numpy as np
3381 a = np.zeros((5, 5))
3382 im = Image.fromarray(a)
3383  
3384 If ``obj`` is not contiguous, then the ``tobytes`` method is called
3385 and :py:func:`~PIL.Image.frombuffer` is used.
3386  
3387 In the case of NumPy, be aware that Pillow modes do not always correspond
3388 to NumPy dtypes. Pillow modes only offer 1-bit pixels, 8-bit pixels,
3389 32-bit signed integer pixels, and 32-bit floating point pixels.
3390  
3391 Pillow images can also be converted to arrays::
3392  
3393 from PIL import Image
3394 import numpy as np
3395 im = Image.open("hopper.jpg")
3396 a = np.asarray(im)
3397  
3398 When converting Pillow images to arrays however, only pixel values are
3399 transferred. This means that P and PA mode images will lose their palette.
3400  
3401 :param obj: Object with array interface
3402 :param mode: Optional mode to use when reading ``obj``. Since pixel values do not
3403 contain information about palettes or color spaces, this can be used to place
3404 grayscale L mode data within a P mode image, or read RGB data as YCbCr for
3405 example.
3406  
3407 See: :ref:`concept-modes` for general information about modes.
3408 :returns: An image object.
3409  
3410 .. versionadded:: 1.1.6
3411 """
3412 arr = obj.__array_interface__
3413 shape = arr["shape"]
3414 ndim = len(shape)
3415 strides = arr.get("strides", None)
3416 try:
3417 typekey = (1, 1) + shape[2:], arr["typestr"]
3418 except KeyError as e:
3419 if mode is not None:
3420 typekey = None
3421 color_modes: list[str] = []
3422 else:
3423 msg = "Cannot handle this data type"
3424 raise TypeError(msg) from e
3425 if typekey is not None:
3426 try:
3427 typemode, rawmode, color_modes = _fromarray_typemap[typekey]
3428 except KeyError as e:
3429 typekey_shape, typestr = typekey
3430 msg = f"Cannot handle this data type: {typekey_shape}, {typestr}"
3431 raise TypeError(msg) from e
3432 if mode is not None:
3433 if mode != typemode and mode not in color_modes:
3434 deprecate("'mode' parameter for changing data types", 13)
3435 rawmode = mode
3436 else:
3437 mode = typemode
3438 if mode in ["1", "L", "I", "P", "F"]:
3439 ndmax = 2
3440 elif mode == "RGB":
3441 ndmax = 3
3442 else:
3443 ndmax = 4
3444 if ndim > ndmax:
3445 msg = f"Too many dimensions: {ndim} > {ndmax}."
3446 raise ValueError(msg)
3447  
3448 size = 1 if ndim == 1 else shape[1], shape[0]
3449 if strides is not None:
3450 if hasattr(obj, "tobytes"):
3451 obj = obj.tobytes()
3452 elif hasattr(obj, "tostring"):
3453 obj = obj.tostring()
3454 else:
3455 msg = "'strides' requires either tobytes() or tostring()"
3456 raise ValueError(msg)
3457  
3458 return frombuffer(mode, size, obj, "raw", rawmode, 0, 1)
3459  
3460  
3461 def fromarrow(
3462 obj: SupportsArrowArrayInterface, mode: str, size: tuple[int, int]
3463 ) -> Image:
3464 """Creates an image with zero-copy shared memory from an object exporting
3465 the arrow_c_array interface protocol::
3466  
3467 from PIL import Image
3468 import pyarrow as pa
3469 arr = pa.array([0]*(5*5*4), type=pa.uint8())
3470 im = Image.fromarrow(arr, 'RGBA', (5, 5))
3471  
3472 If the data representation of the ``obj`` is not compatible with
3473 Pillow internal storage, a ValueError is raised.
3474  
3475 Pillow images can also be converted to Arrow objects::
3476  
3477 from PIL import Image
3478 import pyarrow as pa
3479 im = Image.open('hopper.jpg')
3480 arr = pa.array(im)
3481  
3482 As with array support, when converting Pillow images to arrays,
3483 only pixel values are transferred. This means that P and PA mode
3484 images will lose their palette.
3485  
3486 :param obj: Object with an arrow_c_array interface
3487 :param mode: Image mode.
3488 :param size: Image size. This must match the storage of the arrow object.
3489 :returns: An Image object
3490  
3491 Note that according to the Arrow spec, both the producer and the
3492 consumer should consider the exported array to be immutable, as
3493 unsynchronized updates will potentially cause inconsistent data.
3494  
3495 See: :ref:`arrow-support` for more detailed information
3496  
3497 .. versionadded:: 11.2.1
3498  
3499 """
3500 if not hasattr(obj, "__arrow_c_array__"):
3501 msg = "arrow_c_array interface not found"
3502 raise ValueError(msg)
3503  
3504 schema_capsule, array_capsule = obj.__arrow_c_array__()
3505 _im = core.new_arrow(mode, size, schema_capsule, array_capsule)
3506 if _im:
3507 return Image()._new(_im)
3508  
3509 msg = "new_arrow returned None without an exception"
3510 raise ValueError(msg)
3511  
3512  
3513 def fromqimage(im: ImageQt.QImage) -> ImageFile.ImageFile:
3514 """Creates an image instance from a QImage image"""
3515 from . import ImageQt
3516  
3517 if not ImageQt.qt_is_installed:
3518 msg = "Qt bindings are not installed"
3519 raise ImportError(msg)
3520 return ImageQt.fromqimage(im)
3521  
3522  
3523 def fromqpixmap(im: ImageQt.QPixmap) -> ImageFile.ImageFile:
3524 """Creates an image instance from a QPixmap image"""
3525 from . import ImageQt
3526  
3527 if not ImageQt.qt_is_installed:
3528 msg = "Qt bindings are not installed"
3529 raise ImportError(msg)
3530 return ImageQt.fromqpixmap(im)
3531  
3532  
3533 _fromarray_typemap = {
3534 # (shape, typestr) => mode, rawmode, color modes
3535 # first two members of shape are set to one
3536 ((1, 1), "|b1"): ("1", "1;8", []),
3537 ((1, 1), "|u1"): ("L", "L", ["P"]),
3538 ((1, 1), "|i1"): ("I", "I;8", []),
3539 ((1, 1), "<u2"): ("I", "I;16", []),
3540 ((1, 1), ">u2"): ("I", "I;16B", []),
3541 ((1, 1), "<i2"): ("I", "I;16S", []),
3542 ((1, 1), ">i2"): ("I", "I;16BS", []),
3543 ((1, 1), "<u4"): ("I", "I;32", []),
3544 ((1, 1), ">u4"): ("I", "I;32B", []),
3545 ((1, 1), "<i4"): ("I", "I;32S", []),
3546 ((1, 1), ">i4"): ("I", "I;32BS", []),
3547 ((1, 1), "<f4"): ("F", "F;32F", []),
3548 ((1, 1), ">f4"): ("F", "F;32BF", []),
3549 ((1, 1), "<f8"): ("F", "F;64F", []),
3550 ((1, 1), ">f8"): ("F", "F;64BF", []),
3551 ((1, 1, 2), "|u1"): ("LA", "LA", ["La", "PA"]),
3552 ((1, 1, 3), "|u1"): ("RGB", "RGB", ["YCbCr", "LAB", "HSV"]),
3553 ((1, 1, 4), "|u1"): ("RGBA", "RGBA", ["RGBa", "RGBX", "CMYK"]),
3554 # shortcuts:
3555 ((1, 1), f"{_ENDIAN}i4"): ("I", "I", []),
3556 ((1, 1), f"{_ENDIAN}f4"): ("F", "F", []),
3557 }
3558  
3559  
3560 def _decompression_bomb_check(size: tuple[int, int]) -> None:
3561 if MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS is None:
3562 return
3563  
3564 pixels = max(1, size[0]) * max(1, size[1])
3565  
3566 if pixels > 2 * MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS:
3567 msg = (
3568 f"Image size ({pixels} pixels) exceeds limit of {2 * MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS} "
3569 "pixels, could be decompression bomb DOS attack."
3570 )
3571 raise DecompressionBombError(msg)
3572  
3573 if pixels > MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS:
3574 warnings.warn(
3575 f"Image size ({pixels} pixels) exceeds limit of {MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS} pixels, "
3576 "could be decompression bomb DOS attack.",
3577 DecompressionBombWarning,
3578 )
3579  
3580  
3581 def open(
3582 fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes],
3583 mode: Literal["r"] = "r",
3584 formats: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] | None = None,
3585 ) -> ImageFile.ImageFile:
3586 """
3587 Opens and identifies the given image file.
3588  
3589 This is a lazy operation; this function identifies the file, but
3590 the file remains open and the actual image data is not read from
3591 the file until you try to process the data (or call the
3592 :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Image.load` method). See
3593 :py:func:`~PIL.Image.new`. See :ref:`file-handling`.
3594  
3595 :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or a file object.
3596 The file object must implement ``file.read``,
3597 ``file.seek``, and ``file.tell`` methods,
3598 and be opened in binary mode. The file object will also seek to zero
3599 before reading.
3600 :param mode: The mode. If given, this argument must be "r".
3601 :param formats: A list or tuple of formats to attempt to load the file in.
3602 This can be used to restrict the set of formats checked.
3603 Pass ``None`` to try all supported formats. You can print the set of
3604 available formats by running ``python3 -m PIL`` or using
3605 the :py:func:`PIL.features.pilinfo` function.
3606 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3607 :exception FileNotFoundError: If the file cannot be found.
3608 :exception PIL.UnidentifiedImageError: If the image cannot be opened and
3609 identified.
3610 :exception ValueError: If the ``mode`` is not "r", or if a ``StringIO``
3611 instance is used for ``fp``.
3612 :exception TypeError: If ``formats`` is not ``None``, a list or a tuple.
3613 """
3614  
3615 if mode != "r":
3616 msg = f"bad mode {repr(mode)}" # type: ignore[unreachable]
3617 raise ValueError(msg)
3618 elif isinstance(fp, io.StringIO):
3619 msg = ( # type: ignore[unreachable]
3620 "StringIO cannot be used to open an image. "
3621 "Binary data must be used instead."
3622 )
3623 raise ValueError(msg)
3624  
3625 if formats is None:
3626 formats = ID
3627 elif not isinstance(formats, (list, tuple)):
3628 msg = "formats must be a list or tuple" # type: ignore[unreachable]
3629 raise TypeError(msg)
3630  
3631 exclusive_fp = False
3632 filename: str | bytes = ""
3633 if is_path(fp):
3634 filename = os.fspath(fp)
3635 fp = builtins.open(filename, "rb")
3636 exclusive_fp = True
3637 else:
3638 fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)
3639  
3640 try:
3641 fp.seek(0)
3642 except (AttributeError, io.UnsupportedOperation):
3643 fp = io.BytesIO(fp.read())
3644 exclusive_fp = True
3645  
3646 prefix = fp.read(16)
3647  
3648 # Try to import just the plugin needed for this file extension
3649 # before falling back to preinit() which imports common plugins
3650 ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1] if filename else ""
3651 if not _import_plugin_for_extension(ext):
3652 preinit()
3653  
3654 warning_messages: list[str] = []
3655  
3656 def _open_core(
3657 fp: IO[bytes],
3658 filename: str | bytes,
3659 prefix: bytes,
3660 formats: list[str] | tuple[str, ...],
3661 ) -> ImageFile.ImageFile | None:
3662 for i in formats:
3663 i = i.upper()
3664 if i not in OPEN:
3665 init()
3666 try:
3667 factory, accept = OPEN[i]
3668 result = not accept or accept(prefix)
3669 if isinstance(result, str):
3670 warning_messages.append(result)
3671 elif result:
3672 fp.seek(0)
3673 im = factory(fp, filename)
3674 _decompression_bomb_check(im.size)
3675 return im
3676 except (SyntaxError, IndexError, TypeError, struct.error) as e:
3677 if WARN_POSSIBLE_FORMATS:
3678 warning_messages.append(i + " opening failed. " + str(e))
3679 except BaseException:
3680 if exclusive_fp:
3681 fp.close()
3682 raise
3683 return None
3684  
3685 im = _open_core(fp, filename, prefix, formats)
3686  
3687 if im is None and formats is ID:
3688 # Try preinit (few common plugins) then init (all plugins)
3689 for loader in (preinit, init):
3690 checked_formats = ID.copy()
3691 loader()
3692 if formats != checked_formats:
3693 im = _open_core(
3694 fp,
3695 filename,
3696 prefix,
3697 tuple(f for f in formats if f not in checked_formats),
3698 )
3699 if im is not None:
3700 break
3701  
3702 if im:
3703 im._exclusive_fp = exclusive_fp
3704 return im
3705  
3706 if exclusive_fp:
3707 fp.close()
3708 for message in warning_messages:
3709 warnings.warn(message)
3710 msg = "cannot identify image file %r" % (filename if filename else fp)
3711 raise UnidentifiedImageError(msg)
3712  
3713  
3714 #
3715 # Image processing.
3716  
3717  
3718 def alpha_composite(im1: Image, im2: Image) -> Image:
3719 """
3720 Alpha composite im2 over im1.
3721  
3722 :param im1: The first image. Must have mode RGBA or LA.
3723 :param im2: The second image. Must have the same mode and size as the first image.
3724 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3725 """
3726  
3727 im1.load()
3728 im2.load()
3729 return im1._new(core.alpha_composite(im1.im, im2.im))
3730  
3731  
3732 def blend(im1: Image, im2: Image, alpha: float) -> Image:
3733 """
3734 Creates a new image by interpolating between two input images, using
3735 a constant alpha::
3736  
3737 out = image1 * (1.0 - alpha) + image2 * alpha
3738  
3739 :param im1: The first image.
3740 :param im2: The second image. Must have the same mode and size as
3741 the first image.
3742 :param alpha: The interpolation alpha factor. If alpha is 0.0, a
3743 copy of the first image is returned. If alpha is 1.0, a copy of
3744 the second image is returned. There are no restrictions on the
3745 alpha value. If necessary, the result is clipped to fit into
3746 the allowed output range.
3747 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3748 """
3749  
3750 im1.load()
3751 im2.load()
3752 return im1._new(core.blend(im1.im, im2.im, alpha))
3753  
3754  
3755 def composite(image1: Image, image2: Image, mask: Image) -> Image:
3756 """
3757 Create composite image by blending images using a transparency mask.
3758  
3759 :param image1: The first image.
3760 :param image2: The second image. Must have the same mode and
3761 size as the first image.
3762 :param mask: A mask image. This image can have mode
3763 "1", "L", or "RGBA", and must have the same size as the
3764 other two images.
3765 """
3766  
3767 image = image2.copy()
3768 image.paste(image1, None, mask)
3769 return image
3770  
3771  
3772 def eval(image: Image, *args: Callable[[int], float]) -> Image:
3773 """
3774 Applies the function (which should take one argument) to each pixel
3775 in the given image. If the image has more than one band, the same
3776 function is applied to each band. Note that the function is
3777 evaluated once for each possible pixel value, so you cannot use
3778 random components or other generators.
3779  
3780 :param image: The input image.
3781 :param function: A function object, taking one integer argument.
3782 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3783 """
3784  
3785 return image.point(args[0])
3786  
3787  
3788 def merge(mode: str, bands: Sequence[Image]) -> Image:
3789 """
3790 Merge a set of single band images into a new multiband image.
3791  
3792 :param mode: The mode to use for the output image. See:
3793 :ref:`concept-modes`.
3794 :param bands: A sequence containing one single-band image for
3795 each band in the output image. All bands must have the
3796 same size.
3797 :returns: An :py:class:`~PIL.Image.Image` object.
3798 """
3799  
3800 if getmodebands(mode) != len(bands) or "*" in mode:
3801 msg = "wrong number of bands"
3802 raise ValueError(msg)
3803 for band in bands[1:]:
3804 if band.mode != getmodetype(mode):
3805 msg = "mode mismatch"
3806 raise ValueError(msg)
3807 if band.size != bands[0].size:
3808 msg = "size mismatch"
3809 raise ValueError(msg)
3810 for band in bands:
3811 band.load()
3812 return bands[0]._new(core.merge(mode, *[b.im for b in bands]))
3813  
3814  
3815 # --------------------------------------------------------------------
3816 # Plugin registry
3817  
3818  
3819 def register_open(
3820 id: str,
3821 factory: (
3822 Callable[[IO[bytes], str | bytes], ImageFile.ImageFile]
3823 | type[ImageFile.ImageFile]
3824 ),
3825 accept: Callable[[bytes], bool | str] | None = None,
3826 ) -> None:
3827 """
3828 Register an image file plugin. This function should not be used
3829 in application code.
3830  
3831 :param id: An image format identifier.
3832 :param factory: An image file factory method.
3833 :param accept: An optional function that can be used to quickly
3834 reject images having another format.
3835 """
3836 id = id.upper()
3837 if id not in ID:
3838 ID.append(id)
3839 OPEN[id] = factory, accept
3840  
3841  
3842 def register_mime(id: str, mimetype: str) -> None:
3843 """
3844 Registers an image MIME type by populating ``Image.MIME``. This function
3845 should not be used in application code.
3846  
3847 ``Image.MIME`` provides a mapping from image format identifiers to mime
3848 formats, but :py:meth:`~PIL.ImageFile.ImageFile.get_format_mimetype` can
3849 provide a different result for specific images.
3850  
3851 :param id: An image format identifier.
3852 :param mimetype: The image MIME type for this format.
3853 """
3854 MIME[id.upper()] = mimetype
3855  
3856  
3857 def register_save(
3858 id: str, driver: Callable[[Image, IO[bytes], str | bytes], None]
3859 ) -> None:
3860 """
3861 Registers an image save function. This function should not be
3862 used in application code.
3863  
3864 :param id: An image format identifier.
3865 :param driver: A function to save images in this format.
3866 """
3867 SAVE[id.upper()] = driver
3868  
3869  
3870 def register_save_all(
3871 id: str, driver: Callable[[Image, IO[bytes], str | bytes], None]
3872 ) -> None:
3873 """
3874 Registers an image function to save all the frames
3875 of a multiframe format. This function should not be
3876 used in application code.
3877  
3878 :param id: An image format identifier.
3879 :param driver: A function to save images in this format.
3880 """
3881 SAVE_ALL[id.upper()] = driver
3882  
3883  
3884 def register_extension(id: str, extension: str) -> None:
3885 """
3886 Registers an image extension. This function should not be
3887 used in application code.
3888  
3889 :param id: An image format identifier.
3890 :param extension: An extension used for this format.
3891 """
3892 EXTENSION[extension.lower()] = id.upper()
3893  
3894  
3895 def register_extensions(id: str, extensions: list[str]) -> None:
3896 """
3897 Registers image extensions. This function should not be
3898 used in application code.
3899  
3900 :param id: An image format identifier.
3901 :param extensions: A list of extensions used for this format.
3902 """
3903 for extension in extensions:
3904 register_extension(id, extension)
3905  
3906  
3907 def registered_extensions() -> dict[str, str]:
3908 """
3909 Returns a dictionary containing all file extensions belonging
3910 to registered plugins
3911 """
3912 init()
3913 return EXTENSION
3914  
3915  
3916 def register_decoder(name: str, decoder: type[ImageFile.PyDecoder]) -> None:
3917 """
3918 Registers an image decoder. This function should not be
3919 used in application code.
3920  
3921 :param name: The name of the decoder
3922 :param decoder: An ImageFile.PyDecoder object
3923  
3924 .. versionadded:: 4.1.0
3925 """
3926 DECODERS[name] = decoder
3927  
3928  
3929 def register_encoder(name: str, encoder: type[ImageFile.PyEncoder]) -> None:
3930 """
3931 Registers an image encoder. This function should not be
3932 used in application code.
3933  
3934 :param name: The name of the encoder
3935 :param encoder: An ImageFile.PyEncoder object
3936  
3937 .. versionadded:: 4.1.0
3938 """
3939 ENCODERS[name] = encoder
3940  
3941  
3942 # --------------------------------------------------------------------
3943 # Simple display support.
3944  
3945  
3946 def _show(image: Image, **options: Any) -> None:
3947 from . import ImageShow
3948  
3949 deprecate("Image._show", 13, "ImageShow.show")
3950 ImageShow.show(image, **options)
3951  
3952  
3953 # --------------------------------------------------------------------
3954 # Effects
3955  
3956  
3957 def effect_mandelbrot(
3958 size: tuple[int, int], extent: tuple[float, float, float, float], quality: int
3959 ) -> Image:
3960 """
3961 Generate a Mandelbrot set covering the given extent.
3962  
3963 :param size: The requested size in pixels, as a 2-tuple:
3964 (width, height).
3965 :param extent: The extent to cover, as a 4-tuple:
3966 (x0, y0, x1, y1).
3967 :param quality: Quality.
3968 """
3969 return Image()._new(core.effect_mandelbrot(size, extent, quality))
3970  
3971  
3972 def effect_noise(size: tuple[int, int], sigma: float) -> Image:
3973 """
3974 Generate Gaussian noise centered around 128.
3975  
3976 :param size: The requested size in pixels, as a 2-tuple:
3977 (width, height).
3978 :param sigma: Standard deviation of noise.
3979 """
3980 return Image()._new(core.effect_noise(size, sigma))
3981  
3982  
3983 def linear_gradient(mode: str) -> Image:
3984 """
3985 Generate 256x256 linear gradient from black to white, top to bottom.
3986  
3987 :param mode: Input mode.
3988 """
3989 return Image()._new(core.linear_gradient(mode))
3990  
3991  
3992 def radial_gradient(mode: str) -> Image:
3993 """
3994 Generate 256x256 radial gradient from black to white, centre to edge.
3995  
3996 :param mode: Input mode.
3997 """
3998 return Image()._new(core.radial_gradient(mode))
3999  
4000  
4001 # --------------------------------------------------------------------
4002 # Resources
4003  
4004  
4005 def _apply_env_variables(env: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> None:
4006 env_dict = env if env is not None else os.environ
4007  
4008 for var_name, setter in [
4009 ("PILLOW_ALIGNMENT", core.set_alignment),
4010 ("PILLOW_BLOCK_SIZE", core.set_block_size),
4011 ("PILLOW_BLOCKS_MAX", core.set_blocks_max),
4012 ]:
4013 if var_name not in env_dict:
4014 continue
4015  
4016 var = env_dict[var_name].lower()
4017  
4018 units = 1
4019 for postfix, mul in [("k", 1024), ("m", 1024 * 1024)]:
4020 if var.endswith(postfix):
4021 units = mul
4022 var = var[: -len(postfix)]
4023  
4024 try:
4025 var_int = int(var) * units
4026 except ValueError:
4027 warnings.warn(f"{var_name} is not int")
4028 continue
4029  
4030 try:
4031 setter(var_int)
4032 except ValueError as e:
4033 warnings.warn(f"{var_name}: {e}")
4034  
4035  
4036 _apply_env_variables()
4037 atexit.register(core.clear_cache)
4038  
4039  
4040 if TYPE_CHECKING:
4041 _ExifBase = MutableMapping[int, Any]
4042 else:
4043 _ExifBase = MutableMapping
4044  
4045  
4046 class Exif(_ExifBase):
4047 """
4048 This class provides read and write access to EXIF image data::
4049  
4050 from PIL import Image
4051 im = Image.open("exif.png")
4052 exif = im.getexif() # Returns an instance of this class
4053  
4054 Information can be read and written, iterated over or deleted::
4055  
4056 print(exif[274]) # 1
4057 exif[274] = 2
4058 for k, v in exif.items():
4059 print("Tag", k, "Value", v) # Tag 274 Value 2
4060 del exif[274]
4061  
4062 To access information beyond IFD0, :py:meth:`~PIL.Image.Exif.get_ifd`
4063 returns a dictionary::
4064  
4065 from PIL import ExifTags
4066 im = Image.open("exif_gps.jpg")
4067 exif = im.getexif()
4068 gps_ifd = exif.get_ifd(ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo)
4069 print(gps_ifd)
4070  
4071 Other IFDs include ``ExifTags.IFD.Exif``, ``ExifTags.IFD.MakerNote``,
4072 ``ExifTags.IFD.Interop`` and ``ExifTags.IFD.IFD1``.
4073  
4074 :py:mod:`~PIL.ExifTags` also has enum classes to provide names for data::
4075  
4076 print(exif[ExifTags.Base.Software]) # PIL
4077 print(gps_ifd[ExifTags.GPS.GPSDateStamp]) # 1999:99:99 99:99:99
4078 """
4079  
4080 endian: str | None = None
4081 bigtiff = False
4082 _loaded = False
4083  
4084 def __init__(self) -> None:
4085 self._data: dict[int, Any] = {}
4086 self._hidden_data: dict[int, Any] = {}
4087 self._ifds: dict[int, dict[int, Any]] = {}
4088 self._info: TiffImagePlugin.ImageFileDirectory_v2 | None = None
4089 self._loaded_exif: bytes | None = None
4090  
4091 def _fixup(self, value: Any) -> Any:
4092 try:
4093 if len(value) == 1 and isinstance(value, tuple):
4094 return value[0]
4095 except Exception:
4096 pass
4097 return value
4098  
4099 def _fixup_dict(self, src_dict: dict[int, Any]) -> dict[int, Any]:
4100 # Helper function
4101 # returns a dict with any single item tuples/lists as individual values
4102 return {k: self._fixup(v) for k, v in src_dict.items()}
4103  
4104 def _get_ifd_dict(
4105 self, offset: int, group: int | None = None
4106 ) -> dict[int, Any] | None:
4107 try:
4108 # an offset pointer to the location of the nested embedded IFD.
4109 # It should be a long, but may be corrupted.
4110 self.fp.seek(offset)
4111 except (KeyError, TypeError):
4112 return None
4113 else:
4114 from . import TiffImagePlugin
4115  
4116 info = TiffImagePlugin.ImageFileDirectory_v2(self.head, group=group)
4117 info.load(self.fp)
4118 return self._fixup_dict(dict(info))
4119  
4120 def _get_head(self) -> bytes:
4121 version = b"\x2b" if self.bigtiff else b"\x2a"
4122 if self.endian == "<":
4123 head = b"II" + version + b"\x00" + o32le(8)
4124 else:
4125 head = b"MM\x00" + version + o32be(8)
4126 if self.bigtiff:
4127 head += o32le(8) if self.endian == "<" else o32be(8)
4128 head += b"\x00\x00\x00\x00"
4129 return head
4130  
4131 def load(self, data: bytes) -> None:
4132 # Extract EXIF information. This is highly experimental,
4133 # and is likely to be replaced with something better in a future
4134 # version.
4135  
4136 # The EXIF record consists of a TIFF file embedded in a JPEG
4137 # application marker (!).
4138 if data == self._loaded_exif:
4139 return
4140 self._loaded_exif = data
4141 self._data.clear()
4142 self._hidden_data.clear()
4143 self._ifds.clear()
4144 while data and data.startswith(b"Exif\x00\x00"):
4145 data = data[6:]
4146 if not data:
4147 self._info = None
4148 return
4149  
4150 self.fp: IO[bytes] = io.BytesIO(data)
4151 self.head = self.fp.read(8)
4152 # process dictionary
4153 from . import TiffImagePlugin
4154  
4155 self._info = TiffImagePlugin.ImageFileDirectory_v2(self.head)
4156 self.endian = self._info._endian
4157 self.fp.seek(self._info.next)
4158 self._info.load(self.fp)
4159  
4160 def load_from_fp(self, fp: IO[bytes], offset: int | None = None) -> None:
4161 self._loaded_exif = None
4162 self._data.clear()
4163 self._hidden_data.clear()
4164 self._ifds.clear()
4165  
4166 # process dictionary
4167 from . import TiffImagePlugin
4168  
4169 self.fp = fp
4170 if offset is not None:
4171 self.head = self._get_head()
4172 else:
4173 self.head = self.fp.read(8)
4174 self._info = TiffImagePlugin.ImageFileDirectory_v2(self.head)
4175 if self.endian is None:
4176 self.endian = self._info._endian
4177 if offset is None:
4178 offset = self._info.next
4179 self.fp.tell()
4180 self.fp.seek(offset)
4181 self._info.load(self.fp)
4182  
4183 def _get_merged_dict(self) -> dict[int, Any]:
4184 merged_dict = dict(self)
4185  
4186 # get EXIF extension
4187 if ExifTags.IFD.Exif in self:
4188 ifd = self._get_ifd_dict(self[ExifTags.IFD.Exif], ExifTags.IFD.Exif)
4189 if ifd:
4190 merged_dict.update(ifd)
4191  
4192 # GPS
4193 if ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo in self:
4194 merged_dict[ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo] = self._get_ifd_dict(
4195 self[ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo], ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo
4196 )
4197  
4198 return merged_dict
4199  
4200 def tobytes(self, offset: int = 8) -> bytes:
4201 from . import TiffImagePlugin
4202  
4203 head = self._get_head()
4204 ifd = TiffImagePlugin.ImageFileDirectory_v2(ifh=head)
4205 for tag, ifd_dict in self._ifds.items():
4206 if tag not in self:
4207 ifd[tag] = ifd_dict
4208 for tag, value in self.items():
4209 if tag in [
4210 ExifTags.IFD.Exif,
4211 ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo,
4212 ] and not isinstance(value, dict):
4213 value = self.get_ifd(tag)
4214 if (
4215 tag == ExifTags.IFD.Exif
4216 and ExifTags.IFD.Interop in value
4217 and not isinstance(value[ExifTags.IFD.Interop], dict)
4218 ):
4219 value = value.copy()
4220 value[ExifTags.IFD.Interop] = self.get_ifd(ExifTags.IFD.Interop)
4221 ifd[tag] = value
4222 return b"Exif\x00\x00" + head + ifd.tobytes(offset)
4223  
4224 def get_ifd(self, tag: int) -> dict[int, Any]:
4225 if tag not in self._ifds:
4226 if tag == ExifTags.IFD.IFD1:
4227 if self._info is not None and self._info.next != 0:
4228 ifd = self._get_ifd_dict(self._info.next)
4229 if ifd is not None:
4230 self._ifds[tag] = ifd
4231 elif tag in [ExifTags.IFD.Exif, ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo]:
4232 offset = self._hidden_data.get(tag, self.get(tag))
4233 if offset is not None:
4234 ifd = self._get_ifd_dict(offset, tag)
4235 if ifd is not None:
4236 self._ifds[tag] = ifd
4237 elif tag in [ExifTags.IFD.Interop, ExifTags.IFD.MakerNote]:
4238 if ExifTags.IFD.Exif not in self._ifds:
4239 self.get_ifd(ExifTags.IFD.Exif)
4240 tag_data = self._ifds[ExifTags.IFD.Exif][tag]
4241 if tag == ExifTags.IFD.MakerNote:
4242 from .TiffImagePlugin import ImageFileDirectory_v2
4243  
4244 try:
4245 if tag_data.startswith(b"FUJIFILM"):
4246 ifd_offset = i32le(tag_data, 8)
4247 ifd_data = tag_data[ifd_offset:]
4248  
4249 makernote = {}
4250 for i in range(struct.unpack("<H", ifd_data[:2])[0]):
4251 ifd_tag, typ, count, data = struct.unpack(
  • E203 Whitespace before ':'
4252 "<HHL4s", ifd_data[i * 12 + 2 : (i + 1) * 12 + 2]
4253 )
4254 try:
4255 (
4256 unit_size,
4257 handler,
4258 ) = ImageFileDirectory_v2._load_dispatch[typ]
4259 except KeyError:
4260 continue
4261 size = count * unit_size
4262 if size > 4:
4263 (offset,) = struct.unpack("<L", data)
  • E203 Whitespace before ':'
4264 data = ifd_data[offset - 12 : offset + size - 12]
4265 else:
4266 data = data[:size]
4267  
4268 if len(data) != size:
4269 warnings.warn(
4270 "Possibly corrupt EXIF MakerNote data. "
4271 f"Expecting to read {size} bytes but only got "
4272 f"{len(data)}. Skipping tag {ifd_tag}"
4273 )
4274 continue
4275  
4276 if not data:
4277 continue
4278  
4279 makernote[ifd_tag] = handler(
4280 ImageFileDirectory_v2(), data, False
4281 )
4282 self._ifds[tag] = dict(self._fixup_dict(makernote))
4283 elif self.get(0x010F) == "Nintendo":
4284 makernote = {}
4285 for i in range(struct.unpack(">H", tag_data[:2])[0]):
4286 ifd_tag, typ, count, data = struct.unpack(
  • E203 Whitespace before ':'
4287 ">HHL4s", tag_data[i * 12 + 2 : (i + 1) * 12 + 2]
4288 )
4289 if ifd_tag == 0x1101:
4290 # CameraInfo
4291 (offset,) = struct.unpack(">L", data)
4292 self.fp.seek(offset)
4293  
4294 camerainfo: dict[str, int | bytes] = {
4295 "ModelID": self.fp.read(4)
4296 }
4297  
4298 self.fp.read(4)
4299 # Seconds since 2000
4300 camerainfo["TimeStamp"] = i32le(self.fp.read(12))
4301  
4302 self.fp.read(4)
4303 camerainfo["InternalSerialNumber"] = self.fp.read(4)
4304  
4305 self.fp.read(12)
4306 parallax = self.fp.read(4)
4307 handler = ImageFileDirectory_v2._load_dispatch[
4308 TiffTags.FLOAT
4309 ][1]
4310 camerainfo["Parallax"] = handler(
4311 ImageFileDirectory_v2(), parallax, False
4312 )[0]
4313  
4314 self.fp.read(4)
4315 camerainfo["Category"] = self.fp.read(2)
4316  
4317 makernote = {0x1101: camerainfo}
4318 self._ifds[tag] = makernote
4319 except struct.error:
4320 pass
4321 else:
4322 # Interop
4323 ifd = self._get_ifd_dict(tag_data, tag)
4324 if ifd is not None:
4325 self._ifds[tag] = ifd
4326 ifd = self._ifds.setdefault(tag, {})
4327 if tag == ExifTags.IFD.Exif and self._hidden_data:
4328 ifd = {
4329 k: v
4330 for (k, v) in ifd.items()
4331 if k not in (ExifTags.IFD.Interop, ExifTags.IFD.MakerNote)
4332 }
4333 return ifd
4334  
4335 def hide_offsets(self) -> None:
4336 for tag in (ExifTags.IFD.Exif, ExifTags.IFD.GPSInfo):
4337 if tag in self:
4338 self._hidden_data[tag] = self[tag]
4339 del self[tag]
4340  
4341 def __str__(self) -> str:
4342 if self._info is not None:
4343 # Load all keys into self._data
4344 for tag in self._info:
4345 self[tag]
4346  
4347 return str(self._data)
4348  
4349 def __len__(self) -> int:
4350 keys = set(self._data)
4351 if self._info is not None:
4352 keys.update(self._info)
4353 return len(keys)
4354  
4355 def __getitem__(self, tag: int) -> Any:
4356 if self._info is not None and tag not in self._data and tag in self._info:
4357 self._data[tag] = self._fixup(self._info[tag])
4358 del self._info[tag]
4359 return self._data[tag]
4360  
4361 def __contains__(self, tag: object) -> bool:
4362 return tag in self._data or (self._info is not None and tag in self._info)
4363  
4364 def __setitem__(self, tag: int, value: Any) -> None:
4365 if self._info is not None and tag in self._info:
4366 del self._info[tag]
4367 self._data[tag] = value
4368  
4369 def __delitem__(self, tag: int) -> None:
4370 if self._info is not None and tag in self._info:
4371 del self._info[tag]
4372 else:
4373 del self._data[tag]
4374 if tag in self._ifds:
4375 del self._ifds[tag]
4376  
4377 def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[int]:
4378 keys = set(self._data)
4379 if self._info is not None:
4380 keys.update(self._info)
4381 return iter(keys)
